---
title: "repos setup"
---

`repos setup` reads your `repos.list` file, clones all listed repositories into
the **parent directory** of your current location, creates any missing GitHub
repositories, generates a VS Code workspace file, and optionally configures
GitHub Codespaces authentication.

## Basic Usage

```bash
repos setup
```

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `-f <file>` | Use a different list file (default: `repos.list`) |
| `--public` | Create new repositories as public (default is private) |
| `--codespaces` | Configure Codespaces authentication |
| `-d <path>` | Path to a `devcontainer.json` file (repeatable) |

```bash
# Use a custom list file
repos setup -f my-repos.list

# Create new repos as public
repos setup --public

# Enable Codespaces auth with default devcontainer path
repos setup --codespaces

# Specify one or more devcontainer.json paths
repos setup -d .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
repos setup -d path1/devcontainer.json -d path2/devcontainer.json
```

## GitHub Authentication

`repos setup` needs a GitHub token to create repositories and clone private ones.
Provide it via the `GH_TOKEN` environment variable or via the `gh` CLI:

```bash
export GH_TOKEN="your_personal_access_token"
# or
gh auth login
```

## VS Code Workspace

After `repos setup` completes, a file called `entire-project.code-workspace` is
written in your project directory.  Open it to load all cloned repositories as a
multi-root workspace:

```bash
code entire-project.code-workspace
```

## Codespaces Authentication

Pass `--codespaces` to inject the `GH_TOKEN` Codespaces secret into every
cloned repository that has a `devcontainer.json`.  You can override which
`devcontainer.json` files are targeted with one or more `-d` flags:

```bash
repos setup --codespaces -d .devcontainer/prebuild/devcontainer.json
```

## Repository Layout

Repositories are always cloned to the **parent** of your current directory:

```
workspace/
├── my-project/          # your project (contains repos.list)
├── backend/             # cloned from myorg/backend
├── frontend-develop/    # cloned from myorg/frontend@develop
└── docs/                # cloned from myorg/docs
```
